title: Soy-Ginger Shishito Peppers
subhead: A sweet, smoky, umami-glazed snack that comes together in under 15 minutes...
intro: Shishito peppers are one of my favorite quick snacks or sides — they're smoky, a little sweet, and this recipe takes them up a notch with a simple soy-ginger glaze that comes together in just minutes. One in ten will surprise you with a kick. That's part of the fun.
introHighlight: One in ten will surprise you with a kick.

ingredients.item: Avocado oil (or any high smoke point oil)
Sugar (add more if you like it sweeter)

tip: Microwaving the sauce just 15–20 seconds gives it that beautiful glossy thickness. Don't skip this step!

steps.title: Broil the Peppers
steps.description: Spread your shishito peppers on a baking sheet and drizzle with avocado oil. Pop them under the broiler for about 8 minutes, flipping halfway through. You'll know they're ready when the skins are blistered and a little charred.

steps.title: Whisk Up the Sauce
steps.description: While the peppers broil, mix together the grated ginger, garlic, soy sauce, mirin, sesame oil, sugar, potato starch, and water. Stir well, then microwave 15–20 seconds until it thickens into a glossy sauce.

steps.title: Toss & Enjoy
steps.description: Pour the sauce over your hot, blistered peppers and let them sit for a minute so they soak up all that flavor. Serve right away — they're best warm and a little messy.

banner.title: The 1-in-10 Rule
banner.text: Shishito peppers are generally mild, but roughly one in ten will sneak in a surprising kick. The heat depends on growing conditions — there's no way to tell from the outside. That's half the fun.

variations.title: Make It Spicier
variations.text: Add chili flakes or a dash of sriracha to the sauce before microwaving.

variations.title: Extra Sweet
variations.text: Increase sugar to a tablespoon or sub honey for a teriyaki-style glaze.

variations.title: Serve As a Side
variations.text: Pairs beautifully alongside rice, grilled salmon, or Japanese-style chicken.

variations.title: Garnish Ideas
variations.text: Top with sliced scallions, furikake, or extra sesame oil before serving.

variations.title: Make Ahead
variations.text: Mix the sauce ahead and refrigerate. Broil peppers fresh when ready.

variations.title: Oil Swaps
variations.text: No avocado oil? Grapeseed, sunflower, or refined coconut oil all work great.
